title: Alnylam — Overview
html:
Module 1: Alnylam Account Overview

Governing Thought

Alnylam Pharmaceuticals is ZoomRx's premier RNAi therapeutics account -- a pure-play rare disease company undergoing a historic transformation from niche rare disease player to major cardiology force, driven by AMVUTTRA's ATTR-CM approval in March 2025. The account spans three therapeutic areas (ATTR amyloidosis, AHP, PH1) across four brands (AMVUTTRA, ONPATTRO, GIVLAARI, OXLUMO), with ZoomRx deeply embedded in all three TAs through PET, ATU, PCA, and international multi-country tracking studies. Unlike most accounts where the commercial challenge is brand-vs-brand competition, two of Alnylam's three TAs (AHP and PH1) are fundamentally patient-finding markets where ZoomRx's strategic value lies in diagnosis acceleration and market expansion intelligence, not just competitive messaging optimization. The TTR franchise is the exception -- a fiercely competitive three-way (soon four-way) battle against Pfizer's VYNDAMAX, BridgeBio's ATTRUBY, and potentially AstraZeneca/Ionis's Wainua -- and it drives 83% of Alnylam's revenue guidance.

The Alnylam Landscape: What You Need to Know

Alnylam Pharmaceuticals at a Glance

Alnylam is the pioneer of RNAi (RNA interference) therapeutics -- a platform that silences disease-causing genes at the mRNA level in the liver. Every product in their portfolio uses GalNAc-conjugated siRNA for hepatocyte-targeted delivery. This shared platform creates a distinctive account profile: deep scientific cohesion across brands, a unified commercial infrastructure, and an RNAi identity that shapes messaging across all TAs.

Dimension Detail
Headquarters Cambridge, MA
Ticker ALNY (Nasdaq); Market cap ~$40B+
Therapeutic Areas ATTR Amyloidosis, Acute Hepatic Porphyria (AHP), Primary Hyperoxaluria Type 1 (PH1)
Brands AMVUTTRA, ONPATTRO, GIVLAARI, OXLUMO
2025 Revenue TTR franchise $2,487M (AMVUTTRA $2,314M + ONPATTRO $173M); GIVLAARI + OXLUMO ~$500M
2026 Guidance TTR franchise $4,400-$4,700M (83% growth); Total net product revenue ~$5,300M
2030 Strategy "Alnylam 2030" -- $10B+ revenue target driven primarily by TTR franchise scale
Partnership tenure Long history: numbered projects go back to #01 (Patisiran Launch Tracker) through #31 (AMVUTTRA PET 2026)
Active project types PET, ATU, PCA, International PET (5 countries), Message Recall, Digital/TV Tracking, Integrated Analysis
Meeting cadence Weekly check-in meetings (22+ documented through April 2026)

Why this account is distinctive: Alnylam is one of the few pure-play gene silencing companies in pharma. All four brands share the same RNAi/GalNAc platform, the same rare disease commercial infrastructure, and often the same field representatives. Understanding the RNAi mechanism once unlocks understanding across the entire portfolio. The account is in a once-in-a-generation growth inflection -- AMVUTTRA's ATTR-CM approval (March 2025) transformed the company from a ~$3B rare disease player to a ~$5.3B company with a clear path to $10B+ by 2030.

TTR Amyloidosis Portfolio (Primary Revenue Driver)

The TTR franchise generates ~80% of Alnylam's total revenue and is the company's strategic center of gravity. It encompasses two brands targeting a progressive, multisystem disease caused by misfolded transthyretin protein.

AMVUTTRA (vutrisiran) -- Flagship

  • What it is: GalNAc-conjugated siRNA targeting TTR mRNA; Alnylam's flagship product and the company's growth engine
  • Administration: Subcutaneous injection, once every 3 months (quarterly)
  • Indications: hATTR-PN (polyneuropathy, approved June 2022) and ATTR-CM (cardiomyopathy, approved March 2025 -- transformational)
  • Key differentiator: Only product approved for BOTH ATTR-CM and hATTR-PN; 88% mean TTR knockdown; quarterly SC dosing
  • 2025 Revenue: $2,314 million; >12,000 patients on therapy
  • 2026 Guidance: TTR franchise $4,400-$4,700M (83% growth)
  • Pivotal data: HELIOS-B -- 28% reduction in death + recurrent CV events overall; 33% in monotherapy arm. Significant improvements in 6MWT, KCCQ, NT-proBNP
  • Key competitive dynamic: AMVUTTRA vs. VYNDAMAX (Pfizer tafamidis, $5.45B) vs. ATTRUBY (BridgeBio acoramidis, $362M first year). The cardiologist reach gap is the defining commercial challenge -- Alnylam dominates neurologists but trails Pfizer among cardiologists across all international markets
  • ZoomRx work: PET (US quarterly + International 5-country), ATU, PCA (semi-annual), Message Recall, Integrated Analysis, Strategy studies

Principal note: AMVUTTRA is the most analytically complex brand in the account. The PET data spans 6 countries, the PCA tracks combination therapy patterns (silencer + stabilizer), and the competitive landscape has four manufacturers (Alnylam, Pfizer, BridgeBio, AstraZeneca). The neurologist vs. cardiologist specialty divide is the central insight axis.

ONPATTRO (patisiran) -- Legacy

  • What it is: siRNA targeting TTR mRNA via lipid nanoparticle (LNP) formulation; first RNAi therapeutic ever approved (August 2018)
  • Administration: IV infusion every 3 weeks with premedication
  • Approved indication: hATTR-PN only
  • Status: Being transitioned to AMVUTTRA; revenue declining ($173M in 2025)
  • Relevance: Still tracked in France PET data where it has deep neurologist loyalty. Alnylam reps in France equally prioritize AMVUTTRA and ONPATTRO
  • ZoomRx work: Tracked within International PET (France)

Rare Disease Portfolio

These brands serve ultra-rare patient populations where the commercial challenge is fundamentally different from TTR -- the growth constraint is not competition but the size of the diagnosed patient pool.

GIVLAARI (givosiran) -- Acute Hepatic Porphyria

  • What it is: siRNA targeting ALAS1 mRNA; the only approved prophylactic therapy for AHP
  • Administration: Subcutaneous injection, once monthly
  • Market size: Ultra-rare (~500-1,000 recurrent attack patients in US; 15-year average diagnostic delay)
  • Revenue: GIVLAARI + OXLUMO combined ~$500M in FY2025
  • Key competitor: None. PANHEMATIN (hemin) is used acutely but is not a direct competitor for prophylaxis
  • Pivotal data: ENVISION -- 74% reduction in attack rate; 50% attack-free at 6 months
  • Commercial challenge: Patient-finding market, not a competitive market. The "competitor" is non-diagnosis. Every new AHP diagnosis is a potential GIVLAARI patient
  • Key insight: Disease perception is shifting from episodic attacks to chronic condition -- this unlocks expanded patient populations (Types 4 and 5)
  • ZoomRx work: US PET, Global ATU (US, Germany, Brazil)

Principal note: GIVLAARI's analytics are unusual -- all messages are above rare disease industry average, reps are rated highly, NPS is strong. The problem is not promotional effectiveness but market ceiling. ZoomRx's value here is tracking disease awareness, diagnostic patterns, and HCP segmentation that shows where growth comes from.

OXLUMO (lumasiran) -- Primary Hyperoxaluria Type 1

  • What it is: siRNA targeting HAO1 mRNA (glycolate oxidase); reduces oxalate overproduction in PH1
  • Administration: Subcutaneous injection quarterly (after loading doses); HCP-administered
  • Market size: Ultra-rare (3,000-5,000 estimated US patients; ~5 per million per 2025 genetic prevalence data; dramatically underdiagnosed)
  • Revenue: Part of GIVLAARI + OXLUMO combined ~$500M
  • Key competitor: Rivfloza (nedosiran, Novo Nordisk) -- different MOA target (LDHA), monthly self-administration, restricted to eGFR >=30
  • Pivotal data: ILLUMINATE A/B/C -- 53% UOx reduction sustained through 36 months; approved for ALL ages and ALL kidney stages including dialysis
  • Key differentiator: Only PH1 therapy approved for all ages and all kidney stages (Rivfloza restricted to eGFR >=30)
  • Commercial challenge: Dual -- competitive (OXLUMO vs. Rivfloza) AND patient-finding (massive underdiagnosis). Access/affordability perception gap is the #1 consistent finding across all studies
  • ZoomRx work: PET (head-to-head OXLUMO vs. Rivfloza), ATU

Principal note: The OXLUMO PET is the most direct head-to-head competitive tracker in the Alnylam account. The access/affordability perception gap where HCPs perceive Rivfloza as more affordable even without experience is the single most consistent and actionable insight across 2 years and 4 studies.

Pipeline to Watch

Timeline Asset Impact
Mid-2026 CARDIO-TTRansform (Wainua/eplontersen in ATTR-CM) data readout If positive, creates silencer-vs-silencer competition with AMVUTTRA. If negative, consolidates AMVUTTRA's monopoly
2026 ALN-TTRsc04 Phase 1 data Alnylam's next-gen TTR silencer (potentially 6-month dosing or improved knockdown)
2026-2027 ATTRUBY/BEYONTTRA international expansion (Bayer in EU, Alexion in Japan) Changes international competitive landscape for AMVUTTRA
2028 Tafamidis patent expiration Generic entry risk; potential price disruption across ATTR-CM class
2030 Alnylam 2030 strategy targets $10B+ revenue target; validates the TTR franchise trajectory

ZoomRx's Service Footprint

ZoomRx's engagement with Alnylam spans all three TAs with a comprehensive mix of US and international tracking studies. The relationship is deeply embedded -- weekly check-in meetings (22+ documented through April 2026) and cross-study integrated analysis demonstrate strategic partnership, not transactional vendor status.

Project Type ATTR (AMVUTTRA / ONPATTRO) AHP (GIVLAARI) PH1 (OXLUMO)
PET (Promotional Effectiveness) US quarterly + International 5-country US semi-annual US (head-to-head vs Rivfloza)
ATU (Awareness, Trial, Usage) US quarterly (ATTR-CM + hATTR-PN) Global (US, Germany, Brazil) US (Wave 1 Dec 2024)
PCA (Patient Chart Audit) US semi-annual (CM + Mixed + PN) -- --
International PET France, Italy, Germany, Spain, Japan -- --
Message Recall Patient Message Recall study -- --
Digital/TV Tracking TV Tracking (2024) -- --
Integrated Analysis PET + ATU + PCA integration -- --
Strategy Studies AMVUTTRA 2025 Strategy -- --
Check-In Meetings Weekly (22+ items) Included Included

Key takeaway: ATTR/AMVUTTRA has the deepest project portfolio -- the only TA with PCA, international PET, integrated analysis, and dedicated strategy studies. GIVLAARI and OXLUMO run PET + ATU combinations but without the depth of the TTR engagement. The combined OXL+GIV PET readout covers both rare disease brands in a single deliverable, reflecting the shared field force.

Competitive Landscape Summary

Alnylam faces fundamentally different competitive dynamics in each TA:

TA / Brand Primary Competitor Competitive Dynamic Implication for ZoomRx Work
AMVUTTRA (ATTR-CM) VYNDAMAX (Pfizer tafamidis, $5.45B) Incumbent oral daily vs. SC quarterly silencer. Pfizer has massive cardiology reach advantage. AMVUTTRA winning on clinical data (HELIOS-B mortality); Pfizer winning on convenience and physician familiarity International PET tracks the neurologist-vs-cardiologist divide across 5 countries. US PET tracks four manufacturers. PCA tracks combination therapy patterns
AMVUTTRA (ATTR-CM) ATTRUBY (BridgeBio acoramidis, $362M) "Better stabilizer" narrative. Oral daily. Fastest onset (1 month). Specifically cardiology-native positioning ATTRUBY tracked in US PET with 90 interactions/quarter. PCA already showing AMVUTTRA + ATTRUBY combination charts
AMVUTTRA (ATTR) Wainua (AstraZeneca/Ionis eplontersen) Pending ATTR-CM data (mid-2026). Monthly SC self-administered. Direct silencer-vs-silencer competition if approved Wainua fielding paused in US PET at 103% completion; will resume post-data readout
GIVLAARI (AHP) Non-diagnosis No direct competitor. PANHEMATIN (hemin) is used acutely. Growth comes from finding undiagnosed patients and expanding treated population to chronic symptom patients ATU tracks disease perception shift, HCP segmentation (Enthusiasts/Trialists/Converters/Inexperienced), and diagnostic patterns. PET tracks message effectiveness (all above industry average)
OXLUMO (PH1) Rivfloza (Novo Nordisk nedosiran) Two RNAi therapies targeting different enzymes in the same pathway. OXLUMO has all-ages/all-stages label; Rivfloza has self-administration and access perception advantage PET is head-to-head competitive tracker. ATU captures specialty divergence (nephrology vs. urology) and access perception gap

Bottom line: The AMVUTTRA competitive landscape is by far the most complex -- four manufacturers across cardiologist/neurologist specialties in 6+ countries. GIVLAARI is a patient-finding story, not a competitive story. OXLUMO is a true head-to-head competitive battle but in an ultra-rare market with small sample sizes. Know these three fundamentally different dynamics and do not conflate them.

Alnylam's Strategic Imperatives (2025-2026)

Based on the Q1 2025 Integration of Market Research deck, Alnylam's US TTR Business Unit operates on four strategic imperatives:

# Imperative ZoomRx Studies Feeding It
1 Establish AMVUTTRA as first-line treatment of choice -- Differentiate as new SOC in 1L ATTR-CM; RNAi as MOA of choice; rapid TTR knockdown narrative PET (message effectiveness), ATU (awareness/perceptions), PCA (treatment journey/source of share)
2 Drive urgency to diagnose, treat, and optimize therapy -- Accelerate diagnosis via disease education; support understanding of progression ATU (disease beliefs, tests used), PET (disease state message recall)
3 Optimize customer experience and minimize non-clinical barriers -- Formulary, access, reduce admin/site barriers PET (access message recall), ATU (access/affordability perceptions)
4 Establish leadership in cardiology/ATTR -- Expand presence in cardiology, build champions, pipeline interest PET (cardiologist-specific SOV and perception data), International PET (specialty-specific tracking)

Account Complexity Profile

Alnylam presents a unique account management challenge: three TAs that share a platform and field infrastructure but face completely different market dynamics.

Dimension ATTR (TTR Franchise) AHP (GIVLAARI) PH1 (OXLUMO)
Market type Competitive (4 manufacturers) Patient-finding (no competitor) Competitive + Patient-finding
Revenue scale $4.4-4.7B (2026 guidance) Part of ~$500M combined Part of ~$500M combined
Growth driver ATTR-CM indication expansion + cardiologist conversion Diagnostic acceleration + chronic disease reframing OXLUMO vs. Rivfloza differentiation + underdiagnosis
Primary specialty Cardiology (CM) + Neurology (PN) Hepatology / Hematology / Porphyria specialists Nephrology + Urology
International scope 5-country PET + global commercial Global ATU (US, Germany, Brazil) US only
Key insight axis Neurologist vs. cardiologist divide HCP adoption ladder (4 segments) Nephrologist vs. urologist divergence

This diversity means ZoomRx's deliverables for Alnylam must speak in three different analytical languages depending on the TA, even though the client-side audience may overlap.

Back to Accounts Hub | Home
